---
title: "Documentation"
---

## Getting Started

SimiC-Suite contains two complementary packages:

| Package | Main role | Repository |
|---|---|---|
| SimiCPipeline | Phenotype-specific GRN inference and Regulon Activity Score (RAS) calculation. | <https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCPipeline> |
| SimiCviz | Assessment, comparison, and visualization of GRN outputs. | <https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCviz> |

::: {.package-band .pipeline-band}
## SimiCPipeline

Use SimiCPipeline when the goal is to infer phenotype-specific gene regulatory networks from single-cell RNA-seq data and compute Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values.

Main repository:

<https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCPipeline>
:::

::: {.package-band .viz-band}
## SimiCviz

Use SimiCviz when the goal is to assess, compare, and visualize GRN outputs in R / Bioconductor workflows. SimiCviz is designed to be tool-agnostic, so it can support outputs from different GRN inference approaches.

Main repository:

<https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCviz>
:::

## Suggested Workflow

1. Prepare single-cell RNA-seq data and phenotype annotations.
2. Run SimiCPipeline for phenotype-specific GRN inference.
3. Calculate Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values with SimiCPipeline.
4. Use SimiCviz to assess and visualize GRN outputs.
